steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content: The noun phrase “also known as the greater adjutant” interrupts the main sentence between the subject Leptoptilos dubius and the verb “can be found.” A comma already follows the subject.

steps.title: Apply the appositive rule
steps.content:
Commas must enclose an appositive phrase that interrupts the main clause, so the opening comma (after the subject) requires a closing comma after “adjutant.” The other choices violate this rule with a semicolon (A), no punctuation (B), or an em dash (D).

C is correct.
